description: Golden set for founder-signal detection eval. Researchers who published at NeurIPS (2015-2025) with independently verified founder / non-founder status. Verified via public sources 2026-07 (see evidence_url per entry). Non-founder means: no public founding evidence as of the as_of date; advisor/angel/employee roles do not count.

researchers.name: Aravind Srinivas
researchers.label: founder
researchers.notes: Co-founder & CEO of Perplexity AI (2022, with Yarats, Konwinski, Ho). RAD NeurIPS 2020 authorship confirmed.

researchers.name: Misha Laskin
researchers.label: founder
researchers.notes: Co-founder & CEO of Reflection AI (March 2024, with Ioannis Antonoglou); ex-DeepMind Gemini. Publishes under the byline 'Michael Laskin' — deliberate name-variant hard case. Shares RAD with Srinivas and Abbeel (coauthor-cluster test).

researchers.name: Arthur Mensch
researchers.label: founder

papers.title: Flamingo: a Visual Language Model for Few-Shot Learning

researchers.notes: Co-founder & CEO of Mistral AI (May 2023, with Lample and Lacroix). Flamingo authorship at DeepMind confirmed.

researchers.name: Guillaume Lample
researchers.label: founder
researchers.notes: Co-founder & Chief Science Officer of Mistral AI (April/May 2023). XLM (Conneau & Lample) NeurIPS 2019 confirmed.

researchers.name: Noam Shazeer
researchers.label: founder
researchers.notes: Left Google Oct 2021 to co-found Character.AI (with Daniel De Freitas); returned to Google 2024 in a ~$2.7B deal; later moved to OpenAI. Time-sensitive-evidence hard case.

researchers.name: Aidan Gomez
researchers.label: founder
researchers.notes: Co-founder & CEO of Cohere (2019). Was a 20-year-old Google Brain intern on the Transformer paper; paused Oxford PhD to found Cohere.

researchers.name: David Ha
researchers.label: founder

papers.title: Recurrent World Models Facilitate Policy Evolution

researchers.notes: Co-founder & CEO of Sakana AI (Tokyo, 2023, with Ren Ito and Llion Jones). World Models (Ha & Schmidhuber) NeurIPS 2018 confirmed.

researchers.name: Chelsea Finn
researchers.label: founder
researchers.notes: Stanford professor; co-founder & research lead of Physical Intelligence (2024). PMAML (Finn, Xu, Levine) NeurIPS 2018 confirmed.

researchers.name: Sergey Levine
researchers.label: founder
researchers.notes: UC Berkeley professor; co-founder of Physical Intelligence (2024 founding team: Hausman, Esmail, Ichter, Levine, Finn, Groom, Vuong). Both paper authorships confirmed.

researchers.name: Albert Gu
researchers.label: founder

papers.title: HiPPO: Recurrent Memory with Optimal Polynomial Projections

researchers.notes: Chief Scientist & co-founder of Cartesia (2023, with Karan Goel, Desai, Yang). HiPPO (Gu, Dao, Ermon, Rudra, Ré) NeurIPS 2020 confirmed. Mid-fame difficulty tier.

researchers.name: Yi Tay
researchers.label: founder

papers.title: Transformer Memory as a Differentiable Search Index

researchers.notes: Co-founder & Chief Scientist of Reka AI (2023); later returned to Google DeepMind. DSI NeurIPS 2022 confirmed. Time-sensitive-evidence case.

researchers.name: Pieter Abbeel
researchers.label: founder
researchers.notes: Co-founder, President & Chief Scientist of Covariant (2017); also co-founded Gradescope. Covariant team joined Amazon 2024 — evidence remains public. Both paper authorships confirmed.

researchers.name: Marc G. Bellemare
researchers.label: founder

papers.title: Unifying Count-Based Exploration and Intrinsic Motivation

researchers.notes: Originally drafted as a negative — verification found he is co-founder & Chief Scientific Officer of Reliant AI. Low-publicity founding: a genuinely hard positive. Founding year not confirmed; fill in during next verification pass.

researchers.name: Michael I. Jordan
researchers.label: founder
researchers.notes: Originally drafted as an advisor-trap negative — Anyscale's own launch post names him in the founding group (with Nishihara, Moritz, Stoica), and Crunchbase lists him as Founder. Some press lists only the other three: a genuinely ambiguous founder case, kept as founder per primary source.

researchers.name: Aleksander Madry
researchers.label: non_founder

papers.title: Adversarial Examples Are Not Bugs, They Are Features

researchers.notes: MIT professor, later OpenAI (preparedness) — employee role. No public founding evidence found as of 2026-07. Paper authorship (Ilyas et al.) confirmed.

researchers.name: Been Kim
researchers.label: non_founder
researchers.notes: Interpretability researcher at Google DeepMind. No public founding evidence found as of 2026-07. Paper authorship (Adebayo et al.) confirmed.

researchers.name: Luke Zettlemoyer
researchers.label: non_founder

papers.title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs

researchers.notes: UW professor + Meta AI. No public founding evidence found as of 2026-07. QLoRA (Dettmers, Pagnoni, Holtzman, Zettlemoyer) NeurIPS 2023 oral confirmed.

researchers.name: Yejin Choi
researchers.label: non_founder

papers.title: MAUVE: Measuring the Gap Between Neural Text and Human Text using Divergence Frontiers

researchers.notes: UW → Stanford/NVIDIA. No public founding evidence found as of 2026-07. MAUVE (Pillutla et al.) NeurIPS 2021 Outstanding Paper confirmed.

researchers.name: Chi Jin
researchers.label: non_founder
researchers.notes: Princeton associate professor, on sabbatical at OpenAI (employee). No public founding evidence found as of 2026-07. Paper (Jin, Allen-Zhu, Bubeck, Jordan) confirmed.

researchers.name: Sebastien Bubeck
researchers.label: non_founder
researchers.notes: Microsoft Research VP (Phi models) → OpenAI — employee roles throughout. No public founding evidence found as of 2026-07.

researchers.name: Oriol Vinyals
researchers.label: non_founder

papers.title: Matching Networks for One Shot Learning

researchers.notes: DeepMind VP of research — employee throughout. No public founding evidence found as of 2026-07. Paper (Vinyals, Blundell, Lillicrap, Kavukcuoglu, Wierstra) confirmed.

researchers.name: Yee Whye Teh
researchers.label: non_founder
researchers.notes: Oxford professor + DeepMind researcher. No public founding evidence found as of 2026-07. FIVO (Maddison et al., incl. Teh) NeurIPS 2017 confirmed.

researchers.name: Emma Brunskill
researchers.label: non_founder

papers.title: Provably Good Batch Off-Policy Reinforcement Learning Without Great Exploration

researchers.notes: Stanford professor (RL for education/health). No public founding evidence found as of 2026-07. Official title includes 'Off-Policy' (Liu, Swaminathan, Agarwal, Brunskill) — confirmed.

researchers.name: John Duchi
researchers.label: non_founder
researchers.notes: Stanford professor (optimization/statistics). No public founding evidence found as of 2026-07. Paper (Namkoong & Duchi, NeurIPS 2017 best paper) confirmed.
